technical.lead: AMD Q1 2026 beats: revenue $10.25B, Data Center +57% YoY
technical.body: AMD prints Q1 2026 revenue $10.25B vs $9.85B consensus (+38% YoY) with adjusted EPS $1.37 vs $1.27 consensus (+9.6% beat); Data Center segment lands at $5.78B (+57% YoY) against a $5.56B consensus that had implied a +51.5% pace, driven by EPYC + Instinct GPU shipment ramp; non-GAAP gross margin 55%, GAAP 53%; CEO Lisa Su declares Data Center now the primary driver of revenue and earnings growth, citing strengthening MI450 Series + Helios customer engagement; Q2 2026 guidance sets $11.2B (+/- $300M) vs Street $10.52B, implying +50.6% YoY and +9.1% sequential; AMD shares rise 4.4% after-hours. Cleanest single-vendor confirmation that the AI-accelerator revenue ramp is intact post-MI308 China cliff; the Q2 guide is the operative buyside catalyst and pairs with the standing Blackwell deployment scale (~1,000 NVL72 racks/week) to lock in the duopoly disclosure shape.

citations.label: AMD - AMD Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
Investing.com - AMD rises after hours as 57% surge in data center revenue powers Q1 results beat
ProactiveInvestors - AMD reports Q1 earnings beat driven by data center growth

technical.lead: Anthropic 10-agent financial-services launch + FIS AML co-build
technical.body: Anthropic ships a 10-agent financial-services suite on Claude Opus 4.7 (state-of-the-art 64.37% on Vals AI Finance Agent benchmark) covering pitch builder, meeting preparer, earnings reviewer, model builder, market researcher, valuation reviewer, general ledger reconciler, month-end closer, statement auditor, and KYC screener; the Microsoft 365 add-in surface now spans Excel + PowerPoint + Word with Outlook coming soon (cross-app context carries automatically); new connectors land for Dun & Bradstreet, Fiscal AI, Financial Modeling Prep, Guidepoint, IBISWorld, SS&C IntraLinks, Third Bridge, and Verisk; Moody's MCP app brings credit ratings + data on 600M+ public/private companies natively into Claude; testimonial roster includes Citadel, BNY, Carlyle, Mizuho, Travelers, and Walleye Capital. The same week, FIS (powers ~12% of the global economy) announces an Anthropic-built Financial Crimes AI Agent that compresses AML alert + case investigations from days to minutes, auto-assembling evidence, evaluating against known typologies, and surfacing highest-risk cases with regulatory-grade audit trail; BMO + Amalgamated Bank are first deployment customers, broader availability targeted H2 2026. First single-day vertical-agent product launch with native data-broker MCP integrations across the M365 surface, paired with the first Tier-1 banking-platform vendor + frontier-lab joint product.

technical.body: Cisco announces intent to acquire Astrix Security at ~$400M to plug the AI-agent + non-human-identity (NHI) governance gap; Astrix (founded 2021 by Unit 8200 vets Alon Jackson + Idan Gour) discovers and governs API keys, service accounts, OAuth tokens, and machine-to-machine credentials that AI agents now use to gain access at scale; integration is planned into Cisco Identity Intelligence, Cisco Secure Access, and Duo IAM, covering discovery + lifecycle management for agentic access, threat detection on out-of-scope agent behavior, and centralized secrets management; Cisco's enterprise customer survey shows 85% experimenting with AI agents but only 5% in production, with AI agents + non-human identities outnumbering human users roughly 100:1; pairs with the May 1 RSAC stack (Cisco DefenseClaw + Microsoft Agent 365 GA at $15/user/mo) and the standing CSA framing of identity as Zero Trust's first pillar. First major networking-vendor M&A on the agent-identity-control-plane primitive; three-vendor convergence (Cisco + Microsoft + Astrix) on the same agent-identity-as-first-pillar Zero Trust thesis within one week.

technical.lead: Cloudflare Infire Rust LLM inference engine ships at edge
technical.body: Cloudflare's Rust-based custom inference engine Infire ships across the global edge network, separating input-processing and output-generation onto different optimized systems (prefill/decode disaggregation); benchmark places Infire 7% faster than vLLM 0.10.0 on unloaded H100 NVL machines, with significantly better behavior under real load; the design targets serving more requests with fewer GPUs and lower CPU overhead. The launch establishes a third inference-engine candidate alongside vLLM and SGLang, with edge-network distribution + prefill/decode disaggregation as the differentiator; pairs with the standing SGLang vs vLLM 29% throughput gap on H100 (RadixAttention prefix-cache compounds for shared-context workloads — chatbots, RAG, multi-turn agentic) to harden the production-serving Pareto split: SGLang for shared-prefix / agentic, vLLM for batch / multi-hardware, Infire for edge-distributed serving with prefill/decode separation.

citations.label: Cloudflare Blog - Building the foundation for running extra-large language models
InfoQ - Cloudflare Builds High-Performance Infrastructure for Running LLMs

technical.lead: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 patched + Blackwell hyperscaler scale
technical.body: The longest-running 2026 unpatched LLM-tooling CVE category resolves: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8 RCE via the /v1/rerank endpoint with a malicious GGUF model file) lands a fix on Apr 24 after Apr 20 disclosure via CERT/CC by Stuart Beck; root cause was jinja2.Environment() without sandboxing, replaced with ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment per CERT/CC mitigation guidance; supply-chain risk vector = malicious GGUF distributed via model repositories. Backdrop is the May 2026 industry baseline of ~7,000 internet-exposed MCP servers with roughly half operating without authorization controls and a 2026 AI-CVE forecast of 2,800-3,600 disclosures (31-69% YoY increase). On the hardware side, NVIDIA Blackwell B200 deployment scale settles at ~1,000 NVL72 racks per week (each = 72 GPUs configured for inference throughput), B200 on-demand rental ~$6.04/hr (down ~6% YoY from $6.45/hr), with NVIDIA pre-allocating nearly all B200 production through Q2 2026 to hyperscaler priority; B200 architecture lowered cost per million tokens 15x vs prior generation. Combined with the AMD Q1 print, the two-vendor AI-accelerator duopoly disclosure shape now has both Q1 lines in the public record while the agentic-AI security baseline remains structurally exposed.

citations.label: The Hacker News - SGLang CVE-2026-5760 Enables RCE
NVIDIA Perspectives - Real cost AI scale hyperscaler accelerator economics
Adversa AI - Top Agentic AI security resources May 2026
